event: delivered by emergency cesarean delivery at 31 weeks of pregnancy
Apgar values of 4 and 8 in the first and fifth minutes of life
sent to the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU)
minor respiratory acidosis on blood gas
respiratory distress syndrome ground-glass opacity on X-ray
continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) with 6 positive end-expiratory pressure and 30% FiO2 started
trophic feeding with breast milk started
raised C-reactive protein level of 70 mg/L
suspected relationship between hepatitis and total parenteral nutrition
bilirubin, AST, and ALT levels rose
TSH, T3, T4, and cortisol levels normal
whole genomic sequence sent at 8 weeks of age
bradycardia requiring 3 min of aggressive cardio-respiratory resuscitation
died after 100 days in the hospital
whole exome sequencing results: autosomal dominant ALGS2 caused by a pathogenic heterozygous variant c.1076c>T (Ser359Phe) in the NOTCH2 gene
